subject: English
title: Poem: Trees
summary: This short and simple poem describes the unique characteristics and benefits of various common trees like the Banyan, Peepul, Coconut, Tamarind, Date, Teak, and Mango.

sections.title: Prose: When the Trees Walked (Ruskin Bond)

mcqs.question: Who is the author of 'When the Trees Walked'?
Where did Grandfather serve for many years?
mcqs.answer: He served in the [[Indian Forest Service]].

mcqs.question: Where did he build a bungalow after retirement?
Which tree is called a 'show-off'?
Why do peepul tree leaves spin like tops?
mcqs.answer: To [[attract attention]] and invite you into the shade.

mcqs.question: Why did Grandfather help with the flower garden?
Why did Grandfather plant trees in the jungle?
mcqs.answer: For [[animals and birds]] who need food and shelter.

mcqs.question: What happens if we don't replace cut-down trees?
mcqs.answer: The world will become [[one great desert]].

mcqs.question: Which tree manages to move using aerial roots?
Where did they find a rocky island for planting?
What spectacular color were the blossoms on the island?
When did the author return to Dehradun?
mcqs.answer: Several years after the [[Second World War]].

mcqs.question: What had happened to the island when he returned?
What award did Ruskin Bond receive in 2014?
What award did he receive in 1999?
Which is described as the largest tree?
Which tree quivers in the breeze?
Which tree grows up straight and tall?
mcqs.answer: The [[Coconut]] tree grows up straight and tall.

mcqs.question: What does the tamarind tree give us?
Which tree's leaf is as sharp as a blade?
mcqs.answer: The [[Date's]] leaf is sharp as a blade.

mcqs.question: What does the teak tree give us?
Which tree gives us good fruit?
mcqs.answer: The [[Mango]] tree gives us fruit that is good.

mcqs.question: What is the meaning of 'Quivers'?
What is the meaning of 'Pleasant'?
What are rhyming words?
mcqs.answer: Words that have the [[same sound]] at the end (e.g., tower – power).

mcqs.question: What are describing words in the poem?
mcqs.answer: Words like [[tall, large, sharp, useful, pleasant]].

mcqs.question: When is 'Who' used in a sentence?
mcqs.answer: It is used for the [[subject]] (e.g., He/She).

mcqs.question: When is 'Whom' used in a sentence?
mcqs.answer: It is used for the [[object]] (e.g., Him/Her).

mcqs.question: When do we use 'How many'?
mcqs.answer: For [[countable nouns]] like trees or books.

mcqs.question: When do we use 'How much'?
mcqs.answer: For [[uncountable nouns]] like luggage or water.

mcqs.question: When do we use 'Which'?
When do we use 'What'?
What marks the end of a question?
What marks strong emotion?
What marks a statement or command?
Give the antonym for 'Fast'.
Give the antonym for 'Weak'.
Give the antonym for 'Rough'.
Give the antonym for 'Dull'.
What is a suffix?
mcqs.answer: Letters added at the [[end of a word]] (e.g., lone+ly).

questions.question: Which tree is the largest of all in the poem?
Which tree's leaf is sharp as a blade?
